source.scope: All app/admin/* routes + admin-only shared components + backend admin/reviewer endpoints

items.title: Provider correction-request admin inbox is missing
items.source: apps/api/app/api/v1/portal.py (Stage 2.7-a) ships POST /portal/.../correction-requests writing audit_log + Slack, but no /admin/* GET + UI consumes them. Admins must hand-filter /admin/audit-log by action=correction_request.submitted.
items.description: Providers can submit Tier B correction requests from the portal. Each one writes a row to audit_log and best-effort POSTs to Slack. There is no curated admin queue to review the requests, mark them resolved, or capture the resolution. Today admins discover them via Slack, then must manually navigate the audit log to find context.
items.why_it_matters: Slack is a transient channel and audit_log is not a triage UI. Without a dedicated inbox, valid correction requests can stall, and there is no admin-side aggregate view of how many requests are open, which proveedores raised them, or which fields were disputed.
recommended_fix: Add backend GET /admin/correction-requests with the same status taxonomy as contact-requests (new/reviewed/contacted/closed).
Add PATCH /admin/correction-requests/{id} to move status + capture resolution_note.
Add /admin/correction-requests page mirroring /admin/contact-requests chrome (DataTable + Select + Detail dialog).
Reuse the existing audit_log row as the source of truth — no new table; persist status transitions back to the same row's metadata or a sibling table only if necessary.
acceptance_criteria: An internal_admin can open /admin/correction-requests and see all correction-request audit_log rows with status filter.
Status transitions write a new audit_log row (chain of custody preserved).
Detail dialog shows field, current_value, proposed_value, reason, message, requester user, timestamp.
Slack webhook delivery status visible on the row.
Route is gated by internal_admin role; reviewer-only users do not see the nav entry.
test_plan: Backend: pytest covering GET pagination + PATCH status transitions.
Frontend: vitest mount test asserts table + filter + dialog open/close.
Manual: submit a correction from /portal, confirm it appears in /admin/correction-requests.

items.title: Client edit form lets RFC be edited without confirmation
items.source: apps/web/app/admin/clients/page.tsx:255 (RFC input rendered in both create and edit modes); contrast vendor form which hides RFC in edit mode.
items.description: An internal admin can rewrite a client's RFC inline. The audit log captures the change but no UI guardrail explains the legal weight of the field or asks for a justification. The matching vendor edit form correctly hides RFC on edit; this inconsistency suggests an oversight rather than a deliberate choice.
items.why_it_matters: RFC is a legal identifier. Quiet edits can mask provider/client mix-ups, hide subsequent compliance breaches, and create reconciliation problems with SAT lookups. The audit log is reactive, not preventive.
recommended_fix: Lock the RFC input on edit by default, surface an explicit 'Editar identificador legal' toggle.
When toggled on, require a one-line justification captured as `reason` on the PATCH call.
Write a richer audit_log row with old + new + reason.
Optionally mirror vendor form: keep RFC immutable from the UI, route edits through a dedicated correction-request flow (A-001).
acceptance_criteria: Editing a client without toggling the RFC lock leaves RFC unchanged.
Editing with the toggle and a justification writes an audit_log row that contains the justification and both values.
Empty justification blocks the submit.
test_plan: Frontend: vitest assertion that PATCH is not called when the toggle is off.
Backend: pytest asserting reason is required when rfc changes.
Manual: edit a client RFC, verify audit_log row shape.

items.title: Reviewer detail renders raw rule_code as primary signal title
items.source: apps/web/app/admin/reviewer/[submission_id]/page.tsx:329-358 (ReasonsCard) — `title={r.rule_code}`. Provider equivalent uses a REASON_TITLES map; central helper `lib/constants/validation.ts::validationLabel` exists and is already used by `components/checkwise/validation-summary.tsx`.
items.description: Reviewers see raw snake_case rule_codes (`file_exists`, `pdf_readable_text`, `duplicate_hash`, …) as the primary title of each signal row. Stage 1 BL-003 fixed this on the provider portal but the admin reviewer surface was missed.
items.why_it_matters: Reviewers are not engineers. Reading 'file_exists' next to 'PDF protegido con contraseña' breaks the trust layer the rest of the page builds. It also makes it harder to write reviewer-side QA without inside knowledge of the prevalidation module.
recommended_fix: Import `validationLabel` from `@/lib/constants/validation` in the reviewer detail page.
Render `validationLabel(r.rule_code)` as the row title; keep the raw `rule_code` in the row's `title=` attribute and aria-label for QA traceability.
If a code is not yet in `VALIDATION_RULE_LABELS_ES`, the helper falls back to the raw code so a missing entry is loud, not silent.
acceptance_criteria: Reviewer detail shows 'Texto legible' rather than 'pdf_readable_text' as the primary title.
Hovering the row reveals the raw `rule_code` via the title attribute.
aria-label includes the raw code so screen readers / QA still see it.
test_plan: Vitest snapshot of ReasonsCard against a fixture containing every rule_code in `VALIDATION_RULE_LABELS_ES`.
Manual: open /admin/reviewer/<id> for a submission with mixed warning/error reasons.

items.title: Admin/Client calendar year input floors at 2024, blocks legitimate REPSE years 2021-2023
items.description: The year input on both calendar surfaces enforces `min=2024`, stricter than the backend (`MIN_YEAR=2021`). Admins inspecting historical REPSE periods between 2021 and 2023 cannot type the year — the browser blocks submission.
items.why_it_matters: REPSE compliance starts in 2021. The product story is 'we track everything since the reform.' A UI floor that contradicts that story silently truncates the operational window and contradicts the backend BL-T7 fix.
recommended_fix: Change both inputs to `min={2021}` so the frontend floor matches the backend MIN_YEAR.
Optionally import MIN_YEAR / MAX_YEAR from a shared module to avoid drift.
acceptance_criteria: Year input accepts 2021, 2022, 2023 without browser-side rejection.
Year input still rejects values <2021 and >2030.
Backend call succeeds for 2021 input.
test_plan: Manual: type 2021 in the admin calendar, verify the page loads the snapshot for that year.
Backend: existing tests/test_period_validation.py covers the API side.

items.title: No structured taxonomy of legally meaningful rejection causes
items.source: components/checkwise/admin/review-decision-panel.tsx — `reject` / `request_clarification` / `mark_exception` capture only a free-text reason. The reason is shown to the provider, so consistency matters legally.
items.description: Reviewers can reject with any free text. There is no chip list of common causes (RFC mismatch, periodo incorrecto, sello inválido, opinión incumplida, firma faltante, etc.). This produces inconsistent provider-facing copy and no way to aggregate why rejections happen.
items.why_it_matters: The reason is the legal artifact a provider sees. Inconsistency makes future audits harder; the absence of structured codes makes ops reporting (how many rejections last month cited RFC mismatch?) impossible.
recommended_fix: Add a curated chip list of common causes to ReviewDecisionPanel; selecting a chip pre-populates the textarea with the cause and a starter sentence.
Persist a separate `reason_code` field alongside the free text so the reviewer can still edit but a structured code is captured.
Sign off the chip list with Paco / Beko / legal — do not ship final copy without their approval.
acceptance_criteria: ReviewDecisionPanel shows a chip list when action is reject/clarify/exception.
Selecting a chip pre-populates the textarea; reviewer can still edit.
Backend stores reason_code separately from reason for aggregate reporting.
Legal-approved copy lives in a constants module so future edits are reviewed.
test_plan: Vitest mount test of the chip list.
Backend pytest asserting the new field is persisted and surfaced on subsequent GETs.
Manual: walk through reject flow, confirm provider sees the populated reason.

items.title: Reviewer detail does not surface the 'Acerca de este documento' guidance
items.source: Stage 2 added `anatomy` / `where_to_obtain` / `common_errors` to `compliance_catalog.py` for the provider expediente cards. Reviewers do not see the same context when making a decision.
items.description: When a reviewer opens a submission for a requirement they are not deeply familiar with, they lack the same compliance-catalog guidance the provider got. Giving the reviewer the same anatomy / where-to-obtain / common errors block (collapsed by default) shortens the time to a correct decision.
items.why_it_matters: Reviewer accuracy and speed go up when the source-of-truth reference is one click away. Today reviewers must context-switch to docs or matriz regulatoria.
recommended_fix: On reviewer detail, surface the requirement-level catalog block (already attached to portal cards) as a collapsed `<details>` with anatomy / where to obtain / common errors.
Pull from the same `compliance_catalog` source; no new DB table.
acceptance_criteria: Reviewer can expand 'Acerca de este documento' on /admin/reviewer/<id> and see anatomy / dónde obtenerlo / errores comunes.
Block is empty when the requirement has no catalog entry.
test_plan: Vitest mount test against a fixture with + without catalog entries.
Manual: confirm requirements with catalog entries render guidance.

items.title: Backend /admin/calendar default year is hardcoded to 2026
items.source: apps/api/app/api/v1/admin.py:841 `year: Annotated[int, Query(ge=MIN_YEAR, le=MAX_YEAR)] = 2026`. Mirrors the BL-T3 spirit — hardcoded forward years age silently.
items.description: If a caller omits the year query parameter, the admin calendar serves the 2026 snapshot. In 2027 this will quietly read stale unless the frontend always sets the parameter (which it does today, but the contract should not rely on it).
items.why_it_matters: Defaults that age make demos and integration tests lie about the current state. The BL-T3 fix already removed hardcoded 2026 from provider UI copy; the backend default is the next leak.
recommended_fix: Replace the literal `2026` default with `date.today().year` resolved at request time.
Keep the `ge=MIN_YEAR, le=MAX_YEAR` clamp.
acceptance_criteria: Calling GET /admin/calendar with no year returns the current calendar year's snapshot.
year=2021 still works (clamp lower bound).
year=1945 still 422s (clamp validator unchanged).
test_plan: Backend: extend tests/test_admin_api.py with a year-default assertion (FreezeGun or monkeypatch on datetime.date.today).

items.title: Hardcoded 'REPSE 2026' string in requirement_service fallback
items.source: apps/api/app/services/requirement_service.py:153 `legal_basis="Pendiente de semilla completa desde matriz regulatoria REPSE 2026."`.
items.description: When the catalog has not been seeded for a requirement, the service stamps a fallback `legal_basis` that contains 'REPSE 2026' literally. Surfaces back to admin/legal-facing requirement detail.
items.why_it_matters: Same class of issue BL-T3 fixed in the marketing surfaces. Quietly ages and starts to feel wrong by year-end.
recommended_fix: Replace the literal year with a dynamic string OR drop the fallback string and leave legal_basis null when the catalog is incomplete.
Final copy needs Paco / Beko / legal sign-off.
acceptance_criteria: No literal 'REPSE 2026' string in `app/services/requirement_service.py`.
Pytest covers the fallback path.
test_plan: Backend: assert legal_basis matches the new helper output.

items.title: VALIDATION_RULE_LABELS_ES missing entries for native_intake, canonical_*, submission_replacement
items.source: apps/web/lib/constants/validation.ts vs backend rule_code emitters in `services/submission_service.py` and `services/prevalidation.py`.
items.description: Some rule_codes the backend can emit are not in the central label map: `native_intake`, `canonical_requirement_code`, `canonical_period_key`, `submission_replacement`. They normally have severity=info and are filtered out of reviewer reasons, but the gap exists.
items.why_it_matters: If one of these ever escalates to warning/error severity, the reviewer surface will fall back to the raw code — silent regression of A-003.
recommended_fix: Add Spanish labels for the four missing codes.
Document the map invariant: every rule_code emitter in backend has an entry in `VALIDATION_RULE_LABELS_ES`.
acceptance_criteria: All backend rule_codes have an entry in the label map.
QA can grep both sides and find zero gaps.
test_plan: Manual grep; optional: a build-time script that diffs emitters vs labels.

items.title: Admin dashboard uses 'workspaces' caption in visible signals
items.description: The dashboard description and signal list still use the internal term 'workspaces'. The Stage 1 audit consciously kept 'workspace' as internal vocabulary; this finding is a small clarity tap on the shoulder, not a rewrite.
items.why_it_matters: Non-engineering ops staff sometimes ask what 'workspace' means. A tooltip closes the loop without changing the model.
recommended_fix: Add a one-line tooltip to the 'Workspaces activos' signal: 'Proveedores con expediente activo bajo gestión.'
Do not rename the field in the API.
acceptance_criteria: The signal row exposes a tooltip explaining what an active workspace is.
Other 'workspace' usages on the admin surface remain unchanged.
test_plan: Manual review only.

items.title: Report editor placeholder still references 'mayo 2026'
items.source: apps/web/components/checkwise/reports/editor/report-editor.tsx:566 — placeholder string 'Genera un resumen REPSE de mayo 2026 para los proveedores con SAT pendiente.'
items.description: The AI-prompt placeholder example mentions a specific month-year. Cosmetic, but ages with calendar time.
items.why_it_matters: Same class as A-011. Placeholder example reads stale at year boundaries.
recommended_fix: Replace the literal 'mayo 2026' with a relative phrasing (e.g. 'del mes pasado') or generate the example dynamically from current_date - 1 month.
Keep the rest of the example intact.
acceptance_criteria: Placeholder no longer references a hardcoded month-year.
No translation needed (Spanish copy stays Spanish).
test_plan: Manual review.

items.title: Reviewer eyebrow 'Reviewer workbench' is English-only
items.description: Both reviewer pages use the eyebrow 'Reviewer workbench' in English. Everything else on the page is Spanish. Internal vocabulary tolerated, but flagged for polish.
recommended_fix: Replace with 'Bandeja de revisión' or 'Workbench de revisión' — pick one and apply consistently.
acceptance_criteria: No English-only eyebrows on admin reviewer surfaces.
test_plan: Manual review.

items.title: Confidence and detection-method are not surfaced on reviewer signals
items.source: ValidationEvent rows carry `confidence` and the event type implies the method (pdf_validation / document_intelligence / etc.), but the reviewer detail surfaces neither prominently.
items.description: Reviewers benefit from knowing whether a signal came from a magic-byte check or a heuristic OCR pass, and how confident the system was. Today both pieces are embedded in the timeline but not in the reasons card.
items.why_it_matters: Better calibration of reviewer effort vs auto-detection confidence.
recommended_fix: On the reasons card, add a secondary line: 'Origen: <method> · Confianza: <%>' when the row carries confidence.
Map known event_types to plain Spanish detection labels.
acceptance_criteria: Signals with non-null confidence show it in plain Spanish.
Signals from `document_intelligence` event type are clearly labelled.
test_plan: Vitest mount test against a fixture exercising both states.

items.title: Empty preset gallery copy on /admin/reports is generic
items.source: components/checkwise/reports/list/reports-list-view.tsx:320 — 'Aún no hay reportes disponibles para tu cuenta.'
items.description: When the preset list is genuinely empty (RBAC allows but seed is missing), admins read the same string a provider would see for a missing assignment. A tester-visible hint that says which seed slot is missing would speed QA.
items.why_it_matters: Stage 1 specifically split error vs empty. The remaining empty case could still mislead a tester debugging a missing seed.
recommended_fix: Pass a role-specific empty copy through the ReportsListView props.
For admin: 'No hay plantillas operativas asignadas a tu rol. Confirma con el equipo que el seed esté completo.'
acceptance_criteria: Admin empty copy differs from provider empty copy.
Provider copy unchanged.
test_plan: Vitest snapshot of both branches.

items.title: ReviewDecisionPanel reason placeholder is a single static example
items.source: components/checkwise/admin/review-decision-panel.tsx:271-275 — single placeholder example for the reject path.
items.description: Reviewers see exactly one suggested example sentence. Pair this with A-005 (chip list) — even without the chip taxonomy, rotating examples for each action could improve consistency.
items.why_it_matters: Sets the tone for the rest of the reason; today the example only covers IMSS / period mismatches.
recommended_fix: Add per-action placeholder examples (reject vs clarify vs exception).
Keep the example list short (1-2 per action).
acceptance_criteria: Placeholder copy varies per action.
No legal copy without sign-off.
test_plan: Manual review.

items.title: Reviewer traceability card could be more explicit about diagnostic-only data
items.description: Traceability card surfaces SHA-256, filename, requirement code. It is labeled 'Trazabilidad' but a stronger eyebrow 'Datos técnicos · sólo para diagnóstico' would make the operator separation between business signal and engineering trail clearer.
items.why_it_matters: Reduces the chance a reviewer copies a hash into a provider-facing communication.
recommended_fix: Add a small eyebrow above the card body explaining it is diagnostic-only.
Keep the existing data.
acceptance_criteria: Eyebrow text 'Datos técnicos · sólo para diagnóstico' present above the card data.
Visual structure unchanged.
test_plan: Manual review.
